steps.title: Find the relationship between the sentences
steps.content: Sentences 1–2 describe a challenge: seals must exert energy to dive while keeping their heart rate low to avoid oxygen depletion. Sentence 3 describes the seal's actual behavior: minimal flipper movement to conserve energy. The behavior is a direct consequence of the oxygen challenge, so the blank needs a cause-and-effect signal.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — In the first place,: signals the first item in a series of enumerated points, not a causal consequence. ✗
  • Choice B — On the other hand,: signals contrast, but the seal's energy-conserving behavior follows from the oxygen challenge rather than opposing it. ✗
  • Choice C — For this reason,: directly signals that the minimal flipper movement is a result of the oxygen-related challenge described before. ✓
  • Choice D — In comparison,: signals a comparison between two things, but no second subject is being compared here. ✗
C is correct.
